content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me focus specifically on atom C:9 (carbon with index 9) and identify all its neighbor atoms by examining the bonds connected to it.

From the image, I can see atom C:9 is located in the center-left portion of the molecule. Looking at the connections:

- Atom C:9 is directly bonded to N:8 (nitrogen, index 8) - visible as a blue nitrogen atom connected to C:9
- Atom C:9 is directly bonded to C:7 (carbon, index 7) - visible as a yellow carbon atom to the left of C:9
- Atom C:9 is directly bonded to C:26 (carbon, index 26) - visible as a yellow carbon atom above C:9
- Atom C:9 is directly bonded to C:11 (carbon, index 11) - visible as a yellow carbon atom to the right of C:9

Let me verify these connections once more by carefully examining the bonds in the image. The atom labels show:
- C:9 is connected to N:8 (nitrogen)
- C:9 is connected to C:7 (carbon)
- C:9 is connected to C:26 (carbon)
- C:9 is connected to C:11 (carbon)

Yes, these are the four atoms directly bonded to C:9.

Regarding the geomconf_index, from the file name "geomconf_5446.xyz" and the directory structure, the geomconf_index is 5446.

Based on my analysis of the molecule structure, the neighbor atoms of atom C:9 are:
- Atom index 8, element N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 7, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 26, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 11, element C (carbon)

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 5446.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
